_meta.description: HDB news cards. Edit freely. The 'myTake' field is YOUR voice — leave blank if you don't have a take yet, the card will simply not show that line.

fields.tag: Short label shown as a colored chip on the card image
fields.tagColor: Optional CSS color (hex, var, rgb). Falls back to brand orange.
fields.image: Optional path to image file under Newscard/. Leave blank for gradient placeholder.
fields.title: Headline
fields.myTake: Your 1-line angle on what this means. Optional. Shows as 'Ammon’s take' on the card.

items.title: Pearl’s Hill — Singapore’s Tallest-Ever HDB at 60 Storeys Announced
items.excerpt: ~1,700 flats in Outram — the first public housing on Pearl’s Hill in 40 years. The ‘Shan Shui Hua’ (山水画) design draws on the hill’s heritage, combining a record-breaking height with a rare city-fringe BTO opportunity.

items.title: 13,480 HDB Flats Hit MOP in 2026 — Nearly Double Last Year’s Wave
items.excerpt: A record 13,480 HDB flats are reaching their Minimum Occupation Period this year, up from 6,970 in 2025. Punggol leads with 3,222 units, followed by Queenstown (2,409) and Tampines (2,133). Analysts forecast 26,000–27,000 resale transactions in 2026 with price growth of 2–5%.
items.source: Yahoo News Singapore · Real Estate Asia

items.title: Serenity Park Launches S$505M En Bloc — Freehold OCR Gem
items.excerpt: Freehold Serenity Park (off Yio Chu Kang Road) launched for collective sale at S$505M, closing March 26. Experts predict more en bloc activity — especially from freehold suburban and city-fringe developments looking to unlock value.

items.title: Singapore Tops APAC Safe-Haven Rankings — Private Home Prices to Rise 3% in 2026
items.excerpt: Singapore reclaims top-3 APAC real estate investment status. Private home prices forecast +3%, backed by easing SORA rates (near 1% in Q2), GDP growth of 2.2%, and a 12.7% drop in unsold private stock — the lowest in 15 quarters.

items.title: June 2026 BTO Launch Preview — 6,860 Flats Across 5 Towns
items.excerpt: Includes the first public housing in Lakeview/Shunfu in over 40 years, plus Bukit Merah (1,960 units), Sembawang (2,000 units), Ang Mo Kio and Woodlands. High relevance for anyone eyeing a BTO this year.

items.title: 11 ECs Going Private in 2026 — Owners Set for Big Gains
items.excerpt: The Topiary at Fernvale is up +94.8% since launch price; Citylife@Tampines +89.1%. Once privatised, foreign buyers become eligible — adding liquidity and unlocking significant capital gains for existing owners.

items.title: 2026 New Launch Condo Pipeline — ~20–25 Projects, ~8,400 Units
items.excerpt: 65% of launches are in the OCR (suburban), 19% RCR, and 16% CCR. Mixed-use and integrated developments are dominating — useful for clients deciding whether to wait or buy now.

items.title: Private Condo Rental Market Holds Steady at ~S$4,300/month
items.excerpt: Median rental stable island-wide, with gross yields running 2.5–4% depending on region. Easing SORA rates and renewed expatriate demand are keeping the rental floor firm — good news for investor-minded buyers.
